rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving named things to valid expressions with types; avoid adding new architecture elements.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when diagnostics reference subcomponent paths.
Ensure AGREE scalar types match AADL expectations: int, real, bool; avoid unresolved references to undefined identifiers.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with actual expression types.
Use only visible identifiers from the current AADL scope in AGREE contracts.
Validate that all property associations and references resolve to existing AADL elements before generating AGREE clauses.
Prefer minimal repairs that preserve existing architecture rather than introducing new components or bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for assignments.
AGREE expressions must reference valid identifiers; avoid undefined or package-qualified names not present in the model.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers or types in AGREE contracts that are not already defined in the AADL model.
Do not assume that unresolved references can be fixed by adding new architecture elements; instead, trace and correct the root cause.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Model Analyst agent's role in extracting AADL structure
Requirement analyst's role in compact semantics and traceability
Model fusion agent's role in owner/scope preservation
